variant: give me a comparison of 2 acne treatments, with a timeframe note like 'week 4 — visible change' for each

request.message: give me a comparison of 2 acne treatments, with a timeframe note like 'week 4 — visible change' for each

response_body.response: **adapalene** is faster and more aggressive for acne clearance, but it demands a healthy barrier and SPF discipline. start low (0.025%), use pea-sized on dry skin, expect a 2–4 week purge before week 4–6 payoff. **azelaic acid** is gentler and anti-inflammatory, better if your skin is reactive or you have rosacea mixed in. start 2–3×/week, increase gradually. visible improvement by week 3–4 without the purge. if your barrier is intact and you can commit to SPF, adapalene wins. if you're inflamed or sensitive, azelaic acid first, then layer adapalene later.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Beauty of Joseon Relief Sun Rice + Probiotic SPF 50+ PA++++
products.description: Cosmetic-elegant Asian SPF — chemical filters, no white cast.

visual_blocks.title: two acne treatments: adapalene vs azelaic acid

options.name: adapalene (retinoid)
pros: increases cell turnover, prevents new comedones
works on all acne types (comedonal + inflammatory)
cons: can irritate if barrier is already damaged
avoid if skin is actively inflamed or compromised

options.name: azelaic acid 10–20%
pros: gentler, works on inflammation + bacteria simultaneously
safe to layer, no purge phase
cons: slower than retinoids for severe acne
less effective on purely comedonal acne
may cause mild irritation if overused (start 2–3×/week)
